scenario_title: delete_rr of an absent RR after satisfying prerequisites
test_rationale: This test first proves the owner name is in use via an rrset_exists prerequisite, then issues delete_rr for an RR that is deliberately absent at that owner name. That isolates the underspecified branch in RFC 2136 §3.4.4: whether an absent targeted RR is treated as a no-op success or as a semantic failure. Because the zone is arranged so the update cannot alter any surviving data, the only meaningful oracle is the UPDATE reply RCODE, making it suitable for bind-versus-knot comparison.

group_1.behavior: Treat the absent delete_rr as a successful no-op after prerequisites succeed.
group_1.post_state: miss.campus.edu. still has the original A and TXT rrsets; no change.

group_2.behavior: Treat the absent delete_rr as a semantic failure for the targeted RR and abort the UPDATE.
group_2.post_state: miss.campus.edu. still has the original A and TXT rrsets; no change.

expected_divergence.why_divergence: RFC 2136 §3.4.4 defines how to delete a specific RR if present, but does not specify the response when the targeted RR is absent and prerequisites have already succeeded. §3.4.1/§3.4.2 govern prerequisite failures, not this operation-level no-op vs semantic-failure choice, so implementations may differ between NOERROR and NXRRSET.
